module_mood_contract: earned trust — 'the way I work with agents is something my staff engineer and my CISO can sign off on, before I even try anything big'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+): student leaves the desk while agent works.
Pattern-recognition LOs need n≥2 instances with contrast.
rules_evaluated.evidence: LO 'Discriminate when a job belongs in subagent vs main thread' is satisfied by the Ex1/Ex2 subagent vs Ex3 main-thread explicit contrast named in both the strategy doc and the module structure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The forcing functions ('ask the skill to disclose its weakest part', 'one question at a time', 'name a branch not a rule') are encoded inside fenced prompt blocks. Body prose describes the moves but the fenced prompts carry the mechanics.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text. No authored conversations.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No pair-share, tell-the-room, or discuss-with-neighbor sentences found in student-facing body. Line 24 ('The question, to you: what's the feature...') is addressed to the individual student, not an authored group prompt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: When task selection drives the learning outcome, name both floor AND ceiling explicitly.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 8-9: 'Not a typo-fix, not a quarter-long epic.' Anti-pattern callout present in Prework. Both floor and ceiling named.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Plug points are integration commitments, not labels. UX: operator can paste each prompt without inventing setup.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Plug points in maintainer block (lines 129-134) name 'sponsor-stated ADR home' and 'sponsor-stated team-kit home' — specific org-context types, not vague slots. Body prompts reference scrollback and session artefacts (not invented setup). Fast-operator path: Big Idea → Prework → Start here → Exercise links → Debrief prompt → Ready-to-clear prompt — coherent without re-reading.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 136-139: 'Leap test' sub-section names three outcomes: (1) invokes curated security skill as subagent on real PR — falsifiable via PR comment quoting skill output; (2) writes ADR for architectural decision under named pressure — falsifiable via new file at sponsor-stated ADR path with four standard sections; (3) invokes authored test-strategy skill on a feature without being prompted — falsifiable via scrollback showing no teacher/Nerd cue preceding the invocation. All three are named-verb, own-codebase, falsifiable.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — every produced artefact is a contract row in the maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 141-148: 'Artefact contracts' table covers all three stateful artefacts: ADR (stable path, producing prompt Ex2, consuming modules M4 Phase 2 + Phase 4); test-strategy SKILL.md (~/.claude/skills/test-strategy/SKILL.md, Ex3, M4/M5/M6); CLAUDE.local.md (./CLAUDE.local.md, Debrief sharpen prompt, every future session + M4 onward). Stable identifiers, producing prompts, and consuming modules all named.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch for every forcing function in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 149-158: 'Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch' table covers all six forcing functions across Ex1/Ex2/Ex3/Debrief. Each row names the forcing function, the dominant failure mode, and one named escape hatch (trainer/Nerd move or fallback prompt). Table format satisfies the deterministic grep diagnostic.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured; does not steal the next module's teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Mood target in maintainer block (line 103): 'earned trust'. Strategy doc M3 mood: 'Earned trust — the way I work with agents is something my staff engineer and my CISO can sign off on, before I even try anything big.' Close ('Ready to clear' + /clear) is a literal and emotional clean break into M4's curious readiness. No resolution into M4 territory.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood. Unease is not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M3's mood is earned trust, not unease. Rule applies to unease-mood modules.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode. Big Idea uses positive framing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea (lines 3-6) leads with 'earn your staff engineer's and CISO's trust' — positive discipline framing. References curated skills and authoring as the positive action. No failure-mode title.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity: no front-running downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No skill-authoring terminology from M4/M5/M6 appears prematurely. Subagent primitive introduced here correctly per strategy (M3 is the designated first subagent use). No plan.md, verifier, or three-pattern named in body. 'Bring to Module 4' is a task-sizing callout only, no M4 pedagogy.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts — at least 2-3 theme instances per module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts (lines 34-47) surfaces: Theme 4 (self-aware/grain-of-salt — 'Ask the agent what's weakest about its own output; the mirror reflects what your own read would miss'); Theme 3 (mirror — 'the access-control and STRIDE skills produce a structured read... Comparing your first read to theirs is the thing you name aloud'); Theme 1 (90% correct — 'curated skills from the frontier are strong, not oracular; the hardening decision... and the weak-spot push-back... are where the last 10% earns your judgement'). Three distinct theme instances, correctly phrased in module vocabulary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not standalone section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bolded breadcrumbs at lines 43-47 use the short bolded format within tactical Key Concepts bullets. No standalone 'Themes this module instances:' section. Breadcrumb phrasing uses module vocabulary, not strategy-doc vocabulary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 26: '[Lecture: Skills from the frontier, skills of your own]' placed before exercises. This is a ceiling-showing lecture (demonstrating what curated + authored skills look like before the student attempts them) — an opener by design. Correct placement per rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M3 is not a contrast-mood module. Rule 2 targets contrast-mood modules specifically (M5-style design).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands: 8+/10 at every beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[0].mood_score=8, trace.close.mood_score=8. Both at threshold. Risk flagged (compliance drift at Ex2) is mitigated by documented push-back move in maintainer block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early: M3 earned trust must not bleed into M4 curious readiness.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The /clear prompt (line 79-81) is a hard session boundary. 'Bring to Module 4' (lines 87-91) names task-sizing only, not M4's gap-analysis or send-off pedagogy. 'Next' bridge (lines 83-85) tells what M4 does without teaching it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands: named teaching moment reliably triggers across reasonable persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Primary teaching moment ('ask the skill to disclose its own weakest part, then push back on the critique') is encoded in the fenced Debrief prompt. A speed-runner who skips body still hits the prompt. The invocation-closes-the-loop beat is also in the prompt ('Ask Claude whether the test strategy is any good'). Both forcing functions are in prompts per rule 16.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity: file leads with module's Big Idea or honours its mood contract.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea (lines 3-6) aligns with strategy doc M3 Big Idea. Module leads with the discipline frame ('earn your staff engineer's and CISO's trust') not a failure-mode or compliance frame. 'Team kit accretion starts here' matches strategy doc 'Team kit born here.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: close hands off to M4's open hook.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 83-85 (Next): 'Two signatures, earned... Module 4 turns the discipline inward: memory that reads your system.' Lines 87-91 (Bring to Module 4): 'One or two candidate tasks from your backlog... Not an epic. Not a typo-fix.' Strategy doc M4 open: 'You arrive with one or two candidates you've surfaced yourself.' Hand-off is explicit and symmetric.
